title: Cheap-stop and neutralization proxies
summary: F1 strategy models treat Safety Car/VSC and traffic as major pit-stop timing shocks because they reduce pit-loss opportunity cost.
rationale: F1 strategy models treat Safety Car/VSC and traffic as major pit-stop timing shocks because they reduce pit-loss opportunity cost. The dataset lacks explicit flags, but synchronized abnormal lap times and field compression can proxy these regimes.
implementation_hint: For each Race-LapNumber, create features: fraction of drivers with LapTime_Delta above race quantile, lap-time IQR/median absolute deviation, number of very slow laps, position-change entropy, and lap-to-lap field compression proxy using Position_Change aggregation. Interact these with RaceProgress, TyreLife, and Stint.
expected_effect: Could lift AUC on clustered pit-stop laps caused by safety-car-like or incident-like conditions rather than tyre wear alone.
risk: Some abnormal laps may be pit in/out laps, so proxies can become circular if computed from current lap timing; test versions excluding extreme individual row contribution where possible.
